title: Biochemical Tests for Bacterial Identification
source: MTMI 202 Basic Microbiology, Mahidol University, Asst. Prof. Sirirat Luk-in, PhD

pH_indicators.name: Bromocresol purple
used_for: Glucose O/F test for Gram positive cocci (Staph & Micrococcus)

tests.name: Oxidation-Fermentation (OF) test / Glucose O/F test
tests.purpose: To determine how bacteria utilize carbohydrates (oxidation vs fermentation)

principle.oxidation_aerobic: Glucose → Pyruvate → weak acid (open tube only turns yellow)
principle.fermentation_anaerobic: Glucose → Pyruvate → mixed acid, alcohol, H2, CO2, acetoin (both tubes turn yellow)

tests.agar_type: Semi-solid (to differentiate aerobic vs anaerobic conditions)
tests.procedure: Inoculate two tubes: open tube (O) and sealed tube with paraffin oil (F). Incubate at 35-37°C for 24-48 hr.

+/+.color_change: Both O and F tubes turn Yellow

tests.notes: Use 2 tubes per test (O tube = open, F tube = sealed with paraffin oil)

tests.name: Triple Sugar Iron (TSI) agar test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to utilize glucose, lactose, and sucrose, and to produce Hydrogen sulfide (H2S)

principle.sugar_fermentation: CHO → glucose → pyruvate → mixed acid / weak acid
principle.H2S_production: Sodium thiosulfate → H2S; H2S + ferric ammonium citrate → ferrous sulfide (black precipitation)

main_components.ferric_ammonium_citrate: H2S detection (reacts with H2S → black precipitate)

interpretation_legend.G: Gas production (cracks or bubbles in agar)

interpretation_examples.meaning: Uses glucose only (slant reverts to alkaline from peptone degradation)
Uses glucose and lactose/sucrose with gas

tests.notes: Glucose concentration is only 0.1% (1/10 of lactose/sucrose), so glucose is used up quickly → slant reverts to K. If lactose or sucrose is fermented → entire tube stays A/A.

tests.name: Sugar fermentation test
tests.purpose: To detect sugar fermentation by bacteria
tests.principle: CHO → glucose → pyruvate → mixed acid, alcohol, H2, CO2, acetoin
tests.main_components: Basal medium + specific sugar

interpretation.positive: Color changes to Pink (acid produced)

tests.name: Gas from Glucose test
tests.purpose: To detect gas production from glucose fermentation
tests.principle: Glucose → pyruvate → mixed acid, alcohol, H2, CO2, acetoin
tests.main_components: Basal medium + Glucose + Durham tube (inverted small tube inside)

interpretation.positive_with_gas: Color changes to Pink AND gas bubble trapped in Durham tube
interpretation.positive_without_gas: Color changes to Pink but no gas in Durham tube (acid without gas)

tests.name: Methyl Red (MR) test
tests.purpose: To detect stable mixed acid as end products from glucose fermentation
tests.principle: Glucose → pyruvate → stable mixed acid (lactic, acetic, formic, succinic acid)
tests.main_components: MRVP broth (basal medium + Glucose)
tests.indicator: None in medium (reagent added after incubation)
tests.reagent: Methyl red (MR) solution, 2-3 drops added after incubation

interpretation.positive: Red color (stable mixed acid present, pH ≤4.4)
interpretation.negative: Yellow/orange color (pH too high, no stable acid)

tests.notes: MR and VP tests use the same broth (MRVP broth) but are read differently

tests.name: Voges-Proskauer (VP) test
tests.purpose: To detect acetoin (acetylmethylcarbinol) as end product from glucose fermentation
tests.principle: Glucose → pyruvate → acetoin (butanediol pathway)
tests.main_components: MRVP broth (basal medium + Glucose)
tests.procedure: Add α-naphthol first, then KOH. Mix vigorously. Wait 10-15 min for color development.

interpretation.positive: Red color (acetoin present)

tests.notes: Must add α-naphthol BEFORE KOH. Results read after 10-15 min. MR+/VP- = E. coli; MR-/VP+ = Klebsiella

tests.name: Sugar oxidation test (for NFB)
tests.purpose: To detect sugar oxidation by bacteria
tests.principle: CHO → glucose → pyruvate → weak acids (oxidative pathway only)
tests.main_components: Basal medium + Sugar + agar (semi-solid)
tests.procedure: ALWAYS include a Control tube (no sugar, N source only) alongside Test tube (with sugar). Stab inoculate.

interpretation.positive: Color changes to Yellow (compared to green control)
interpretation.negative: No color change or remains blue/green (same as control)

tests.critical_note: Control tube is mandatory — interpretation is always compared to control

tests.name: Cystine Tryptic Agar (CTA)-Sugar test
tests.purpose: To detect sugar oxidation for identification of Neisseria species
tests.principle: CHO → glucose → pyruvate → weak acids
tests.main_components: CTA basal medium + Sugar + solid agar

interpretation.positive: Color changes to Yellow

tests.name: Citrate test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to utilize citrate as the sole carbon and energy source
tests.principle: Sodium citrate (C source) → cell growth → uses NH4H2PO4 (N source) → produces NH3 (alkaline)
tests.main_components: Simmon's Citrate Agar (sodium citrate, ammonium salts, agar - solid)

interpretation.positive: Growth visible + color changes to Blue (alkaline from NH3 production)

tests.precaution: Avoid large inoculum → may cause False Positive (FP)

tests.name: Malonate test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to utilize malonate as carbon source
tests.principle: Sodium malonate (C source) → cell growth → uses (NH4)2SO4 (N source) → produces NaOH (alkaline)
tests.main_components: Malonate-PD broth (combination test: sodium malonate + ammonium salts + phenylalanine)

interpretation.positive: Color changes to Blue (alkaline)

tests.combination_note: This is a combination test (Malonate-PD broth) — also tests Phenylalanine Deaminase (PD) in same tube
tests.precaution: Avoid large inoculum → may cause False Positive (FP)

tests.name: Phenylalanine deaminase (PD) test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to deaminate phenylalanine to phenylpyruvic acid
tests.principle: Phenylalanine → (deaminase) → Phenylpyruvic acid + Ammonia; Phenylpyruvic acid + FeCl3 → green complex
tests.main_components: Malonate-PD broth + Phenylalanine (combination test)
reagents: 1 N HCl (1 drop) — added first to turn medium yellow
tests.procedure: After incubation, add 1N HCl (1 drop), then 10% FeCl3 (4-5 drops). Read immediately.

interpretation.positive: Changes from yellow to dark green (color fades within 3-5 min — read quickly!)

tests.important_note: Color fades in 3-5 minutes — must read result immediately after color develops

tests.name: Indole test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to deaminate tryptophan to indole by tryptophanase
tests.principle: Tryptophan → (tryptophanase) → Indole + Pyruvate + Ammonia; Indole + DMAB (p-dimethylaminobenzaldehyde) → Red-violet (Ehrlich reaction)
tests.main_components: Tryptone broth (rich in tryptophan)
tests.reagent: Kovacs' indole reagent: 5% DMAB in isoamyl alcohol-HCl (2 drops); store in brown bottle at 4°C
tests.procedure: Add 2 drops of Kovacs' reagent to surface of broth after incubation. Do not shake.

interpretation.positive: Red-violet ring forms on surface

tests.storage: Kovacs' reagent stored in brown bottle at 4°C

tests.name: Lysine Decarboxylase (LD) / Ornithine Decarboxylase (OD) / Arginine Dihydrolase (AD) test
tests.purpose: To determine ability of bacteria to decarboxylate specific amino acids to alkaline diamine products under acid and anaerobic conditions

principle.glucose_fermentation: First: glucose fermentation → Acid (medium turns yellow initially)
principle.result: If enzyme present: alkaline amines override acid → medium turns back to Purple

main_components.glucose: For initial fermentation (to create acid + anaerobic condition)
main_components.amino_acid: L-Lysine / L-Ornithine / L-Arginine (specific for each test)
main_components.NaCl_1_percent: Add 1% NaCl for Vibrio spp. testing

procedure: Prepare Control tube (no amino acid, glucose only) AND Test tube (amino acid + glucose)
Add paraffin oil to seal (anaerobic condition)

interpretation.control_tube: MUST turn Yellow (confirms glucose fermentation occurred)
interpretation.test_positive: Purple color (enzyme present — alkaline overcomes acid)

tests.critical_note: If control tube does NOT turn yellow → test is INVALID (organism did not ferment glucose)

tests.name: Catalase test (Slide catalase test)
tests.purpose: To detect catalase enzyme production
tests.principle: 2H2O2 → (catalase) → 2H2O + O2 (oxygen gas = bubbles)
tests.reagent: 3% Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), 1 drop; store in brown bottle at 4°C
tests.procedure: Place colony on glass slide or in test tube. Add 1 drop of 3% H2O2. Observe for bubbles.

interpretation.positive: Many (vigorous) bubbles produced immediately
interpretation.negative: No bubbles or very few bubbles

false_positive_causes: Using colony from Blood Agar (BA) — RBCs contain catalase → FP
tests.precautions: Use non-iron loops; do not pick colony from Blood agar

tests.name: Coagulase tube test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to convert fibrinogen to fibrin by coagulase enzyme
tests.principle: Fibrinogen → (coagulase) → fibrin clot
tests.main_components: EDTA-rabbit plasma OR (sodium fluoride/EDTA-human plasma + TSB broth = 1:1 ratio)
tests.procedure: Add 1 full loop of bacteria to plasma-broth mixture. Incubate at 35-37°C.
tests.reading_time: Read at 4 hours AND at 18-24 hours

interpretation.positive: Fibrin clot grade +3 to +4

tests.name: Bile esculin hydrolysis test
applicable_organisms: differentiates Group D from non-Group D
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to produce esculinase enzyme AND tolerate 10-40% bile
tests.principle: Esculin → (esculinase) → esculetin + glucose; esculetin + ferric citrate → black phenolic iron complex
tests.main_components: Basal medium + Esculin + Bile Salts (10-40%) + Ferric citrate + agar (solid)

interpretation.positive: Color changes to Black (both bile tolerance AND esculin hydrolysis required)

tests.name: Pyrrolidonyl Arylamidase (PYR) test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to produce Pyrrolidonyl arylamidase (pyrrolidonyl aminopeptidase)
tests.principle: L-pyrrolidonyl-β-naphthylamide → (L-pyrrolidonyl arylamidase) → β-naphthylamide; β-naphthylamide + DMACA → Red color (Schiff base)
tests.main_components: Basal medium + 0.01-0.02% L-pyrrolidonyl-β-naphthylamide (available as PYR agar / PYR broth / PYR strip)
tests.reagent: 0.01% p-dimethylaminocinnamaldehyde (DMACA); store in brown bottle at 4°C
tests.procedure: Inoculate PYR medium. After incubation, add DMACA reagent. Wait 1-2 minutes.

interpretation.positive: Red color develops within 1-2 minutes
interpretation.negative: No color change or other colors

tests.name: Oxidase test
tests.purpose: To detect the presence of cytochrome c oxidase enzyme
tests.principle: TMPD (colorless) + O2 → (cytochrome c oxidase) → Indophenols (purple/dark blue) + H2O
tests.reagent: 1% Tetramethyl-p-phenylenediamine dihydrochloride (TMPD / Kovács oxidase reagent); store in brown bottle at 4°C
tests.procedure: Apply reagent to filter paper or directly to colony on plate. Observe color change.

interpretation.positive: Color changes to dark purple within 5-10 seconds
interpretation.negative: No color change, or color takes longer than 10 seconds to appear

tests.precautions: Use fresh reagents and fresh culture. Use plastic loops (not iron/metal).

tests.name: Urease test
tests.purpose: To detect urease enzyme production
tests.principle: Urea + H2O → (urease) → CO2 + ammonia (alkaline → pH increases)
tests.main_components: Christensen's urea agar (2% urea + peptone + agar - solid)
tests.incubation: 35-37°C, 4-24 hr (rapid urease producers turn positive within 4 hr)

interpretation.positive: Color changes to Pink-red (alkaline from ammonia)

tests.name: Nitrate reduction test
tests.purpose: To determine the ability of bacteria to reduce nitrate (NO3) to reduced products by nitrate reductase

principle.pathway: NO3 → (nitrate reductase) → NO2 → (nitrite reductase) → NO → N2O → N2
principle.detection: NO2 + Sulfanilic acid (A) + α-naphthylamine (B) → Red precipitate (Prontosil)

tests.main_components: Basal medium + Potassium nitrate + Durham tube

reagents.zinc_dust: Added if A+B shows no color — Zn catalyzes remaining NO3 → NO2 (confirms whether NO3 was reduced beyond NO2 or not reduced at all)

procedure: 2. Check Durham tube for gas (N2 gas = complete reduction)
3. Add Solution A (5 drops), then Solution B (5 drops)
4. If no red color appears: add Zinc dust

positive_NO3_to_N2.color_after_AB: No red color (NO3 completely reduced to N2, no NO2 remaining)

positive_NO3_to_other.after_zinc: No color (NO3 was reduced to other products beyond NO2)

negative_NO3_not_reduced.after_zinc: Red color appears (Zn reduced remaining NO3 → NO2, confirming bacteria did NOT reduce NO3)

QC_reference.Pseudomonas_aeruginosa: + (No color after A+B, no color after Zn = NO3 → N2)

tests.name: 6.5% Sodium chloride (NaCl) test
tests.purpose: To determine salt tolerance ability of bacteria by growth in 6.5% NaCl medium
tests.principle: Growth in 6.5% NaCl medium → uses glucose → acid production (indicator changes)
tests.main_components: 6.5% NaCl + basal medium + glucose

interpretation.positive: Growth (turbidity), color may change to yellow

tests.key_organism: Only Enterococcus grows in 6.5% NaCl among catalase-negative cocci

tests.name: Growth in NaCl-peptone water (various concentrations)
purpose: To differentiate Vibrio spp. from Aeromonas spp.
To determine salt tolerance ability across different NaCl concentrations
tests.principle: Growth on different percentages of NaCl-peptone water → Turbidity
tests.main_components: Peptone water with NaCl at concentrations: 0%, 1%, 3%, 6%, 8%, 10%

interpretation.positive: Turbid broth (growth)

tests.precautions: Direct colony inoculation should NOT be used (too heavy). Prepare diluted inoculum.

key_patterns.Note: Aeromonas grows at 0% NaCl; most Vibrio do not

tests.name: Growth at 42°C test
tests.purpose: To determine thermotolerance ability of bacteria
tests.principle: Growth at elevated temperature (42°C)
tests.main_components: Tryptic Soy Agar (TSA) or nutrient agar

interpretation.positive: Growth visible on agar

tests.name: Novobiocin susceptibility test
tests.purpose: To differentiate CoNS and presumptively identify S. saprophyticus (novobiocin-resistant)
tests.principle: Novobiocin (NV, 5 µg disk) inhibits most CoNS except S. saprophyticus which is resistant

interpretation.susceptible_S: Inhibition zone ≥ 16 mm → Sensitive (S) → NOT S. saprophyticus
interpretation.resistant_R: Inhibition zone < 16 mm → Resistant (R) → Presumptive S. saprophyticus

tests.name: Bacitracin susceptibility test
purpose: To differentiate beta-hemolytic Streptococcus and presumptively identify Group A (S. pyogenes)
To differentiate Micrococcus spp. from Staphylococcus spp.
tests.principle: Bacitracin (BC, 0.04 units disk) inhibits Group A beta-hemolytic Streptococcus and Micrococcus spp.

interpretation.susceptible_S: Inhibition zone > 6 mm → Sensitive (S) → Presumptive Group A Streptococcus or Micrococcus
interpretation.resistant_R: Inhibition zone ≤ 6 mm → Resistant (R) → Not Group A

tests.name: Optochin susceptibility test
tests.purpose: To differentiate alpha-hemolytic Streptococcus and presumptively identify S. pneumoniae
tests.principle: Optochin (OP, 5 µg disk) inhibits S. pneumoniae but not viridans streptococci
tests.critical_note: MUST incubate in candle jar (~5% CO2) — without CO2 results may be invalid

interpretation.susceptible_S: Inhibition zone ≥ 14 mm → Sensitive (S) → Presumptive S. pneumoniae
interpretation.resistant_R: Inhibition zone < 14 mm → Resistant (R) → NOT S. pneumoniae (viridans streptococcus)

tests.name: Proteose Peptone Rice agar (PPR) test
purpose: To determine ability of pigment production under specific conditions
To differentiate Group B beta-hemolytic Streptococcus from other Streptococcus
tests.principle: Group B Streptococcus (S. agalactiae) produces orange pigment on starch media under anaerobic condition
tests.main_components: Peptone + Proteose Peptone Rice agar (developed by Milintangura S.) — replaces Granada medium and Islam medium

interpretation.positive: Orange pigment visible along stab line

tests.name: King P / King F pigment test
purpose: To determine the ability of particular pigment production

principle.King_P: King B agar → Pyocyanin (blue-green water-soluble pigment)
principle.King_F: King A agar → Fluorescein/pyoverdin (yellow-green fluorescent under UV light)

tests.main_components: Basal medium + magnesium + phosphate + glycerol + NO iron (iron suppresses fluorescein production)

interpretation.positive: Growth with characteristic color pigment (blue-green for King P; yellow-green fluorescent for King F)

tests.name: Motility test
tests.purpose: To determine the motility of bacteria (presence of flagella)
tests.principle: Motile bacteria move away from stab line through semisolid medium via flagella
tests.main_components: 0.4% agar → Semi-solid agar
tests.procedure: Stab inoculate once in the center of semisolid agar using a straight inoculating needle

interpretation.positive: Turbid/hazy area extending outward from central stab line (diffuse growth)
interpretation.negative: Growth confined to sharp stab line only (no spreading)

special_notes.Listeria_monocytogenes: Shows characteristic 'umbrella-shaped' motility (grows best at room temperature)

tests.name: MIO (Motility, Indole, Ornithine decarboxylase) combination test
tests.purpose: Combined test for motility, indole production, and ornithine decarboxylase in one tube
tests.principle: Combines: Motility (M) in semi-solid, Indole (I) from tryptophan, Ornithine decarboxylase (O) from ornithine
tests.main_components: 0.4% agar (semi-solid) + Casein peptones (tryptophan source) + 0.5% ornithine + 1% glucose
tests.procedure: Stab once in center with needle. Incubate. Then add Kovacs' indole reagent (3-4 drops) for indole reading.

motility_M.positive: Turbid spreading away from stab line
motility_M.negative: Growth along sharp stab line only

indole_I.positive: Red ring on surface after adding Kovacs' reagent

tests.name: Lysine Iron Agar (LIA) test
tests.purpose: Combined test for lysine deaminase (PD-like), lysine decarboxylase (LD), and H2S production
tests.principle: Combines: Lysine deaminase (slant), Lysine decarboxylase (butt), H2S production (black precipitate)
tests.main_components: LIA medium containing lysine, iron salts
tests.procedure: Streak slant AND stab butt with needle

lysine_deaminase_slant.negative: No change at slant (K = alkaline/purple or yellow)

tests.report_format: Deaminase (slant color R/K/A) / LD (butt color K/A) / H2S (+/-)

coagulase_negative.label: CoNS (Coagulase-negative Staphylococci)
coagulase_negative.special_note: If OD+ among CoNS → suspect S. lugdunensis

rows.organism: Streptococcus not gr. A, B, D

quality_control.when_to_perform: Every time a new Media lot is opened or freshly prepared

steps.name: Gross physical appearance
steps.description: Visual inspection of media before use (color, clarity, any contamination)

steps.name: Sterility test
steps.description: Randomly select 5-10% of media → incubate at 35-37°C for 18-24 hr + at RT for 48 hr. No growth expected.

steps.name: Performance test / Usage control
steps.description: Test media performance using ATCC standard strains with appropriate inoculation method

inoculation_methods_for_QC.method_D: Direct colony inoculation (note: this may cause FP for some tests)

QC_ATCC_strains_reference.negative_control: Other streptococci not Group D (no change)

common_false_results_summary.cause: Using colony from Blood agar (RBCs contain catalase)
Large inoculum (carry-over of C from bacterial mass)
Color fades in 3-5 min — read immediately after adding FeCl3

IMViC_pattern_summary.description: Classic IMViC test = Indole / Methyl Red / Voges-Proskauer / Citrate
